meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Learn how to build SaaS pricing logic and overcome tutorial hell. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first real-world project.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start shipping. By building a functional SaaS pricing engine, you move from passive consumption to active engineering. This guide leverages Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, allowing you to focus on the architecture and logic of real-world billing systems.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks watching YouTube tutorials, feeling like you understand the code, only to find your mind blank the moment you open a blank IDE. This is 'tutorial hell,' a purgatory of passive observation that kills developer confidence. You aren't learning; you are merely consuming.

Today, we break the cycle. We are moving beyond CRUD apps and building a production-grade SaaS pricing module. By integrating AI-assisted workflows, you will transition from a student watching videos to a developer shipping features that solve actual business problems. Let's build.

steps.title: Define the Billing Schema
steps.description: Establish the data structure for your pricing tiers using a modular approach that separates user roles from subscription plans.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers with boolean flags for feature access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a new pricing_schema.ts file based on the generated interface'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic
steps.description: Build the core function that calculates billing intervals and feature gating based on the user's current subscription status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust function to validate if a user has access to a specific feature based on their subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit pricing_logic.ts 'Implement the validation function provided'

steps.title: Simulate Stripe Integration
steps.description: Create a mock service to handle payment intent creation, simulating real-world Stripe webhook responses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock Stripe service in Node.js that handles subscription creation events and returns a mock session URL.

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI Component
steps.description: Develop a responsive React pricing table that dynamically updates based on the application state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Tailwind CSS pricing card component that toggles between monthly and yearly billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate PricingTable.tsx component with Tailwind styles'

steps.title: Testing the Edge Cases
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate unit tests that specifically target subscription upgrades and billing failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest unit tests for the pricing engine, covering scenarios like user upgrade, downgrade, and expired sessions.

pro_tips: Always build your billing logic as a standalone service; this makes it portable across any framework you use later.
Use environment variables for all pricing IDs; never hardcode plan IDs directly into your logic layer.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic early; it is a standard industry practice that users appreciate during payment failures.
When using AI, ask for 'defensive code' to ensure your pricing engine throws clear errors rather than silent failures.
Document your API contracts before coding; it forces you to think like a system architect rather than a script kiddie.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully built the pricing engine, deploy your app to Vercel or Netlify and connect a real Stripe test key to observe the full end-to-end flow.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready pricing module and the workflow to scale it. Don't let this knowledge sit idle; ship it, break it, and iterate. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
